blocks.content: This guide walks you through the complete delivery workflow — from picking up a shipment to completing the delivery and recording proof.
Always verify your identity in the app before starting your shift.

items.title: Check your assigned shipments
items.description: Open the app and review today's delivery list in the Shipments tab.

items.title: Arrive at the pickup location
items.description: Navigate to the sender's address. Enable location services for geofence verification.

items.title: Scan or enter the tracking number
items.description: Use the QR scanner or type the tracking number manually.

items.title: Record proof of pickup
items.description: Take a photo or short video of the package before loading it.

items.title: Navigate to the delivery address
items.description: Use the map link in the shipment details.

items.title: Arrive within geofence
items.description: You must be within 100 meters of the box location for delivery to be accepted.

items.title: Upload delivery proof
items.description: Record a short video or take a photo of the package being placed in the box.

items.title: Enter the door code
items.description: After the proof is accepted, the delivery code will appear. Enter it to open the box.

items.title: Confirm delivery
items.description: Mark the shipment as delivered in the app.

blocks.label: Important
blocks.content: The delivery code only appears after your proof video/photo is successfully uploaded. Do not skip the upload step.

items.question: What if the box door doesn't open?
items.answer: Wait 30 seconds and try again. If it still doesn't work, contact support via the floating button in the app.

items.question: What if I can't reach the geofence?
items.answer: Make sure GPS is enabled. If the location is genuinely incorrect, take a photo of the address and contact support.

items.question: Can I leave the package outside the box?
items.answer: No. All deliveries must go inside the box. If the box is full, mark the shipment as failed and contact support.

items.question: What is an oversized shipment?
items.answer: A shipment too large for the upper slot. It goes through the lower door, which requires a separate passcode.
